title: Lumina Role Definition Schema V1
description: JSON Schema for Project Lumina RBAC role records. Defines the structure for each canonical role in the system.

role_id.description: Unique canonical identifier for the role.

role_name.description: Human-readable display name for the role.

hierarchy_level.description: Position in the role hierarchy. 0 = highest (root), 3 = lowest (user). Peers share the same level.

description.description: Purpose and responsibilities of this role.

default_module_mode.description: Recommended default octal mode when this role owns a module. Only applicable to roles that can own modules (root, domain_authority).

bypass_permissions.description: When true, this role bypasses all permission checks. Only valid for the root role.

scope_type.description: How this role's access scope is determined. global = all modules; governed_modules = per JWT governed_modules claim; assigned_modules = per ACL; session_only = own sessions only.

max_session_scope.description: Maximum session visibility. all = every session in the system; governed = sessions in governed modules; assigned = sessions in ACL-assigned modules; own = only sessions the user created.

audit_visibility.description: System Log record visibility scope. Determines which audit trails this role can inspect.

may_create_users.description: Whether this role can register new users.

may_assign_roles.description: Whether this role can change other users' roles.
